data.narrative:
HyImpulse is a Series B space and aerospace company founded by Mario Kobald that has raised €74M to date. The valuation assessment draws from a universe of 4 same-stage private peers in the space and aerospace sector: Aalyria ($1.3B), Stoke Space ($1.0B), Hubble Network ($400M), and Sift ($274M). A public comp universe of 6 aerospace tickers exists (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) with a median price-to-sales multiple of 45.3x, though this method is not applicable without disclosed target revenue.

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach. The median valuation across the four same-sector, same-stage peers is $700M, with a range spanning $274M to $1.3B. This method reflects direct comparability to companies at identical development stage and sector exposure. No public-comp anchoring is possible given the absence of disclosed revenue for HyImpulse.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $595M to $805M, centered on $700M. This narrow range reflects reliance on a single applicable method across a limited but relevant peer set. Key uncertainties include the relative maturity and commercial traction of HyImpulse versus named peers, capital efficiency relative to the €74M raised, and technological differentiation within the crowded space-tech financing environment. A wider valuation band would be justified absent additional operational or financial metrics.
